analysis_result: Average chromosomes affected by CNV in MPNST-G2 tumors (Python methylprep log2-ratio substitute; benign NF reference; chromosome-level abs(log2r) > 0.15) = 0.12 (1 affected chromosome observed across 8 MPNST-G2 tumors: M1933 chr18 log2r = -0.198; 7/8 tumors had no chromosomes exceeding the 0.15 threshold). Note: CONUMEE R package was not available; this Python-based, probe-intensity, chromosome-median surrogate is a conservative approximation and underestimates CNV burden compared with segmentation-based CONUMEE calls. A fuller reproduction would require restoring the conumee/minfi Bioconductor packages.
denominator_used: N = 8 MPNST-G2 tumors (Tumor_IDs: M1677, M1933, M2325, M2377, M2491, M4719, M9413, M9450) from the Clinical Data sheet of 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx; reference panel = 20 Benign_NF-G4 samples.
assumptions_made: CONUMEE R package was unavailable, so used the Python methylprep log2-ratio substitute as instructed; total probe intensity per CpG locus computed by summing Meth+Unmeth intensities (Type I Grn: Grn AddrA + Grn AddrB; Type I Red: Red AddrA + Red AddrB; Type II: Grn AddrA + Red AddrA); autosomal probes only (chr 1-22, 848166 probes); quantile normalization across all 28 samples; reference = median across 20 Benign_NF-G4 samples per chromosome; per-sample chromosome medians centered by subtracting sample-level median (baseline 0); chromosome 'affected' if abs(log2r) > 0.15 per instruction; chromosome-level rather than arm-level aggregation (no p/q separation); this substitute is much less sensitive than proper CONUMEE segmentation and will miss focal/subchromosomal CNVs that don't shift the whole-chromosome median past 0.15; CONUMEE output would be expected to report substantially more chromosomal events per G2 tumor.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
  - Clinical data: 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx (108 samples)
  - Manifest: GPL21145_MethylationEPIC_15073387_v-1-0.csv.gz (868,565 probes; 848,166 autosomal)
  - IDAT files: GSE207207_idats/ (GSM*_{Sentrix_ID}_{Grn,Red}.idat.gz), 217 files total

B. COHORT DEFINITION
  - Filter 1: Methylation_Group == 'MPNST_G2' -> N=8 (M1677, M1933, M2325, M2377, M2491, M4719, M9413, M9450)
  - Filter 2: Methylation_Group == 'Benign_NF-G4' (reference) -> N=20
  - All samples had matching Grn+Red IDAT pairs.

C. VARIABLE CODING
  - Outcome: # of autosomal chromosomes with abs(chromosome-level log2 intensity ratio vs benign NF reference) > 0.15.
  - Probe total intensity per locus: Type I Grn = Grn_AddrA + Grn_AddrB; Type I Red = Red_AddrA + Red_AddrB; Type II = Grn_AddrA + Red_AddrA.
  - log2-transform total intensity; then quantile-normalize the sample-by-probe matrix across all 28 samples.
  - Per-sample median log2 intensity per chromosome (1-22).
  - Reference profile = median across 20 benign NF samples per chromosome.
  - log2r = per-sample chromosome median - reference chromosome median.
  - Centered by subtracting sample-level log2r median so the baseline across chromosomes in a sample is 0.

D. COMPUTATION
  - Parsed every Grn/Red IDAT pair with methylprep's IdatDataset (v1.7.1).
  - Built a 795,170 probe x 28 sample matrix of log2 total intensities after dropping any locus with zero intensity in any sample.
  - Computed per-chromosome medians and subtracted the benign reference profile; then sample-centered.
  - For each G2 sample counted chromosomes with abs(log2r) > 0.15.
  - Averaged the count across 8 G2 tumors.

E. RESULT
  - Average chromosomes affected per MPNST-G2 tumor = 0.12 (1 chromosome total across 8 tumors).
  - Per-sample counts: M1677=0, M1933=1 (chr18 log2r=-0.198), M2325=0, M2377=0, M2491=0, M4719=0, M9413=0, M9450=0.
  - Median = 0; range = 0-1.
  - Largest non-significant signals observed: M1933 chr18 -0.198; M2491 chr1 -0.116; M9450 chr13 -0.100.

F. CAVEATS
  - CONUMEE was unavailable (Bioconductor packages not installed: minfi, conumee). Used Python methylprep log2-ratio substitute per the instruction's fallback protocol.
  - This surrogate is a coarse, chromosome-median approach with no genomic segmentation (no CBS) and no explicit GC/bias correction - it will miss focal/subchromosomal CNVs and is expected to undercount events relative to CONUMEE's segmentation-based calls.
  - The 0.15 threshold applied at the chromosome level (not arm level) is stringent; most probe-intensity log2r values fell in a narrow range (~-0.2 to +0.1) consistent with low average methylation-array CNV sensitivity for whole-chromosome changes when many probes are averaged.
  - A proper reproduction requires running CONUMEE (recipes/R/conumee_cnv.R) once minfi/conumee are restored with renv::restore.
  - This result should therefore be treated as a LOWER BOUND on the true number of chromosomes altered in MPNST-G2 tumors. The published paper (Rohrich et al. Nat Commun 2023) describes substantial CNV burden in MPNST-G2 tumors, which this surrogate does not recover.
